manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are a language wizard who likes to guess words by using the given rules.

Welcome to Wordle! You have six attempts to guess the target word, a valid English word of five lowercase letters (a-z). Please use the tags "explanation:" and "guess:" to provide a concise explanation for each guess.

To help you make an informed guess, you will receive a clue for the word, such as
clue: snowy white.

Here is an example guess based on the clue:
explanation: In the fairy tail Snow White, the girl is killed because she eats a poisoned apple. And the word apple has 5 letters.
guess: apple

After each guess, your answer will be validated, and you will receive feedback indicating which letters are correct (green), which letters are correct but in the wrong position (yellow), and which letters are incorrect (red). This feedback can be useful in determining which letters to include or exclude in your next guess.

For example, the feedback for "apple" might be:
guess_feedback: a<yellow> p<yellow> p<green> l<yellow> e<red>

The explanation should contain details about how both the guess_feedback and clue work together in tandem to formulate a new guess.

Do not generate any other text.

Let's begin with your first guess.

clue: longtime supreme court name

content.thinking:
The clue is "longtime supreme court name". I need to think of Supreme Court justices who served for a long time and whose names are 5 letters.

Some notable long-serving Supreme Court justices:
- John Marshall (served 34 years) - Marshall has 8 letters
- William O. Douglas (served 36 years) - Douglas has 7 letters
- Stephen Breyer - Breyer has 6 letters
- Ruth Bader Ginsburg - Ginsburg has 8 letters
- Antonin Scalia - Scalia has 6 letters
- John Paul Stevens - Stevens has 7 letters
- Sandra Day O'Connor - O'Connor has 8 letters (without apostrophe)
- Anthony Kennedy - Kennedy has 7 letters
- Byron White - White has 5 letters! White served for 31 years (1962-1993)

Let me think of other possibilities:
- Black (Hugo Black) - 5 letters, served 34 years
- Stone (Harlan Stone) - 5 letters
- Rehnquist - too long
- Brennan - 7 letters

I think "white" or "black" are good candidates. Byron White served from 1962-1993 (31 years). Hugo Black served from 1937-1971 (34 years).

Let me go with "white" as my first guess.

content.text:
explanation: The clue references a longtime Supreme Court justice. Byron White served on the Supreme Court for 31 years (1962-1993), making him one of the longest-serving justices. His last name "white" is exactly 5 letters.

guess: white

clem_player.response:
explanation: The clue references a longtime Supreme Court justice. Byron White served on the Supreme Court for 31 years (1962-1993), making him one of the longest-serving justices. His last name "white" is exactly 5 letters.

guess: white

explanation: The clue references a longtime Supreme Court justice. Byron White served on the Supreme Court for 31 years (1962-1993), making him one of the longest-serving justices. His last name "white" is exactly 5 letters.

guess_feedback: w<red> h<red> i<red> t<red> e<red>

explanation: details
guess: word

content.thinking:
All letters in "white" are red, so none of those letters (w, h, i, t, e) are in the target word.

Let me think of other longtime Supreme Court justices:
- Rehnquist (too long)
- Scalia (6 letters)
- Ginsburg (8 letters)
- Stevens (7 letters)
- O'Connor (8 letters)
- Breyer (6 letters)
- Souter (6 letters)
- Kennedy (7 letters)
- Thomas (6 letters)
- Black (5 letters) - Hugo Black served 34 years
- Douglas (7 letters)
- Brennan (7 letters)

Hugo Black served from 1937-1971, which is 34 years. "BLACK" is 5 letters and doesn't contain any of the excluded letters (w, h, i, t, e).

Wait, "black" contains h, which was marked red. Let me reconsider.

Actually, looking at "black": b-l-a-c-k. None of these are w, h, i, t, e. So "black" should work.

content.text:
explanation: Since all letters in "white" are incorrect, I need to think of another longtime Supreme Court justice. Hugo Black served on the Supreme Court for 34 years (1937-1971), making him one of the longest-serving justices in history. The word "black" has 5 letters and contains none of the eliminated letters (w, h, i, t, e).
